prompt: Tell me a bio of Michael Collins
expected_answer: Factual accuracy of birth date (October 31, 1930), nationality (American), profession (astronaut, test pilot), Apollo 11 role as Command Module Pilot (1969), NASA astronaut group selection (third group, 1963), Gemini 7 backup role. Dates, mission names, and official roles must be precisely correct.
actual_output:
Provided detailed biographies of both famous Michael Collins figures:

1. **Michael Collins (1890–1922)** — Irish revolutionary, Director of Intelligence of the IRA during the War of Independence, Minister for Finance, Chairman of the Provisional Government of the Irish Free State. Famous for guerrilla warfare strategy and "Bloody Sunday" assassinations. Negotiated the Anglo-Irish Treaty. Killed in an ambush at Béal na Bláth during the Irish Civil War, aged 31.

2. **Michael Collins (1930–2021)** — American astronaut who flew the Apollo 11 command module Columbia around the Moon (1969) while Armstrong and Aldrin made the first lunar landing. Also flew Gemini 10 (1966), becoming the first person to perform more than one spacewalk. Later served as Director of the National Air and Space Museum. Awarded Presidential Medal of Freedom and Congressional Gold Medal. Died aged 90 in Naples, Florida.

All facts sourced from Wikipedia articles.

final_output:
Since no preference was specified, I'll provide biographies of **both** famous Michael Collins figures, as they are both historically significant.

---

# Michael Collins — Irish Revolutionary (1890–1922)

## Overview

Michael Collins (Irish: *Mícheál Ó Coileáin*; 16 October 1890 – 22 August 1922) was an Irish revolutionary, soldier, and politician who was one of the leading figures in the early-20th-century struggle for Irish independence. He served as Director of Intelligence of the Irish Republican Army (IRA) during the War of Independence, Minister for Finance in the self-declared Irish Republic, and Chairman of the Provisional Government of the Irish Free State from January 1922 until his death in an ambush during the Irish Civil War in August 1922. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (Irish leader)]`

## Early Life

Collins was born in **Woodfield, Sam's Cross, near Rosscarbery, County Cork**, on 16 October 1890, the youngest of eight children. His father, **Michael John Collins** (1815–1897), was a farmer, amateur mathematician, and member of the Irish Republican Brotherhood (IRB). His father died when Michael was just six and a half years old. He was a bright, precocious child with a fiery temper and a strong sense of Irish patriotism. A local blacksmith, **James Santry**, and his headmaster at Lisavaird National School, **Denis Lyons** (a member of the IRB), were the first to inspire his nationalist sentiments. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (Irish leader) → Early years]`

At age 15, Collins took the British Civil Service examination and moved to **London** in 1906, where he became a clerk in the Post Office Savings Bank at Blythe House. He later worked as a messenger at a stockbroking firm, Horne and Company, and studied law at **King's College London** (though he did not finish). In London, he joined the **London GAA**, through which he was introduced to the **Irish Republican Brotherhood (IRB)** by **Sam Maguire**, a fellow Corkman. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (Irish leader) → Early years]`

## Easter Rising (1916)

Collins returned to Ireland in January 1916 and participated in the **Easter Rising**, serving as **Joseph Plunkett's aide-de-camp** at the rebellion's headquarters in Dublin's General Post Office (GPO). The Rising was crushed after six days. Collins was arrested but fortuitously avoided harsher treatment or execution — an escape historian Tim Pat Coogan described as "one of the luckiest escapes of his life." He was interned at **Frongoch internment camp** in Wales, where he emerged as a key organizer and began planning for the next phase of the independence struggle. He was released in December 1916. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (Irish leader) → Easter Rising]`

## Rise to Power (1917–1918)

After his release, Collins rose rapidly through the ranks of both **Sinn Féin** and the **Irish Volunteers**. He was elected as **MP for South Cork** in the December 1918 general election, in which Sinn Féin swept the polls across Ireland. The elected Sinn Féin members refused to take their seats at Westminster and instead established the **First Dáil** (Irish parliament) in Dublin in January 1919, declaring the independence of the Irish Republic. Collins was appointed **Minister for Finance**. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (Irish leader) → First Dáil]`

## War of Independence (1919–1921)

Collins played a pivotal role during the Irish War of Independence:

- **Director of Intelligence of the IRA**: He built a vast intelligence network that penetrated deep into the British administration.
- **Guerrilla warfare strategist**: He directed a campaign of hit-and-run tactics against British forces, avoiding the massive casualties of conventional warfare.
- **"The Squad"**: He created a special assassination unit to target British agents and informers. The most famous operation was **"Bloody Sunday"** on 21 November 1920, when Collins's Squad assassinated key British intelligence agents in coordinated morning raids across Dublin. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (Irish leader) → War of Independence]`
- The British offered **£10,000** (equivalent to approximately £570,000 or €645,000 in 2025) for information leading to his capture, but he evaded arrest throughout the war.
- He organized the **National Loan**, raising nearly **£400,000** to fund the Irish Republic. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (Irish leader) → Minister for Finance]`

His nickname was **"The Big Fellow"**, established in his teens, long before he became a political or military leader. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (Irish leader) → Early years]`

## Anglo-Irish Treaty and Civil War

After the July 1921 ceasefire, Collins was one of five plenipotentiaries sent to **London** to negotiate peace terms. The resulting **Anglo-Irish Treaty**, signed in December 1921, established the **Irish Free State** but required an oath of allegiance to the Crown — a provision deeply divisive among Irish republicans. Collins viewed the treaty as offering **"the freedom to achieve freedom"** and helped persuade a majority of the Dáil to ratify it. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (Irish leader)]`

A **Provisional Government** was formed under Collins's chairmanship in January 1922. However, the treaty split the republican movement, leading to the **Irish Civil War**. Collins served as **commander-in-chief of the National Army** from July 1922.

## Death

On **22 August 1922**, Michael Collins was **shot and killed in an ambush by anti-Treaty forces** at **Béal na Bláth, County Cork**. He was just 31 years old. He is buried at **Glasnevin Cemetery, Dublin**. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (Irish leader)]`

## Legacy

Collins is widely regarded as one of the most important figures in modern Irish history. His guerrilla warfare strategies influenced revolutionary movements worldwide. His life was depicted in the 1996 film **"Michael Collins"** starring Liam Neeson. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (film)]`

---

# Michael Collins — American Astronaut (1930–2021)

## Overview

Michael Collins (October 31, 1930 – April 28, 2021) was an American astronaut, test pilot, and major general in the U.S. Air Force Reserve. He is best known for flying the **Apollo 11 command module *Columbia*** around the Moon in 1969 while his crewmates **Neil Armstrong** and **Buzz Aldrin** made the first crewed lunar landing. He was the **fourth person to perform a spacewalk** and the **first person to perform more than one spacewalk**. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (astronaut)]`

## Early Life

Collins was born on **October 31, 1930, in Rome, Italy**, where his father, **James Lawton Collins**, was serving as the U.S. military attaché. He came from a distinguished military family — his father was a career U.S. Army officer, his uncle was **General J. Lawton Collins** (Chief of Staff of the U.S. Army), and his older brother was **James Lawton Collins Jr.** For the first 17 years of his life, Collins moved frequently as his father was posted to various locations, including Rome, Oklahoma, New York, Maryland, Ohio, Puerto Rico, Texas, and Virginia. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (astronaut) → Early life]`

He graduated from **St. Albans School** in Washington, D.C., in 1948, and entered the **United States Military Academy at West Point**, graduating in 1952 (185th of 527 cadets) with a Bachelor of Science degree. He chose the **U.S. Air Force** over the Army to avoid perceptions of nepotism. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (astronaut) → Early life]`

## Military Career

Collins completed flight training and became a **fighter pilot**, flying **F-86 Sabres** at Chambley-Bussières Air Base in France. He won first prize in a 1956 gunnery competition. During a NATO exercise that year, he was forced to **eject from an F-86** after a fire started behind the cockpit. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (astronaut) → Fighter pilot]`

He applied to the **USAF Experimental Flight Test Pilot School** at Edwards Air Force Base, graduating in Class 60C alongside future astronauts **Frank Borman**, **Jim Irwin**, and **Tom Stafford**. He later graduated from the **Aerospace Research Pilot School (ARPS)** Class III. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (astronaut) → Test pilot]`

He married **Patricia Mary Finnegan** from Boston in 1957. They had three children, including actress **Kate Collins**. Patricia passed away in 2014. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (astronaut)]`

## NASA Career

### Selection

Collins first applied for NASA's second astronaut group in 1962 but was not selected. He was later selected as part of **NASA's third group of 14 astronauts** in October 1963, alongside colleagues such as **Ed White**, **David Scott**, and others. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (astronaut) → Space program]`

### Gemini 10 (1966)

Collins's first spaceflight was **Gemini 10** (July 18–21, 1966), with command pilot **John Young**. During this three-day mission, Collins and Young:

- Performed **orbital rendezvous with two Agena target vehicles** and docked with one
- Reached a new altitude record of **475 miles (764 km)** above Earth
- Collins conducted **two spacewalks (EVAs)** — becoming the **first person to perform more than one spacewalk**. During the second EVA, he used a nitrogen gun for maneuvering and recovered a micrometeorite experiment from an abandoned Agena spacecraft
- Completed nearly all major mission objectives

`[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (astronaut) → Gemini 10]`

### Apollo 11 (1969)

Collins's most historic mission was **Apollo 11** (July 16–24, 1969). While Armstrong and Aldrin descended to the lunar surface in the Lunar Module *Eagle*, Collins remained in lunar orbit aboard the **Command Module *Columbia***, orbiting the Moon **thirty times** alone. He was the **second person to orbit the Moon alone** (after John Young on Apollo 10). During each pass behind the Moon, he was completely out of radio contact with both Earth and his crewmates — further from any other human being than anyone had ever been. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (astronaut)]`

Collins was originally assigned as Lunar Module Pilot for an earlier Apollo crew, but was moved to the **Command Module Pilot** position on Apollo 9 (later redesignated Apollo 8, and ultimately Apollo 11) due to his spaceflight experience. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (astronaut) → Apollo program]`

## Post-NASA Career

After retiring from NASA in 1970, Collins:

- Served as **Assistant Secretary of State for Public Affairs** (1970–1971) under President Richard Nixon
- Became **Director of the National Air and Space Museum** (1971–1978), overseeing the museum's construction and its hugely popular 1976 opening
- Served as **Undersecretary of the Smithsonian Institution** (1978)
- Became **Vice President of LTV Aerospace** (1980–1985)
- Started his own consulting firm in 1985

`[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (astronaut)]`

## Awards and Honors

- **Presidential Medal of Freedom** (1969)
- **Congressional Gold Medal** (2011)
- **NASA Distinguished Service Medal**
- **Distinguished Flying Cross**
- **Legion of Merit**
- **Air Force Distinguished Service Medal**

`[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (astronaut)]`

## Death

Michael Collins died on **April 28, 2021**, at age 90, in **Naples, Florida**. He is buried at **Arlington National Cemetery**. `[来源: Wikipedia — Michael Collins (astronaut)]`

---

Both men left extraordinary legacies — one in the fight for Irish independence, the other in humanity's journey to the Moon.
